synopsis: John Kim appealed a decision of Texas Presbytery to the Judicial Commission. The Commission found Mr. Kim's explanation for his absence not satisfactory under BCO 43-10, and the case was deemed abandoned. The Commission voted 12-4 that the absence explanation was not satisfactory.
notes: The decision text clearly appears in section 5-86 of the Fifth GA minutes. Kim was represented by Texas Presbytery members Messrs. Robert Schmitz and Lardner Moore. Neither Mr. Kim nor counsel were present at the hearing. The chair noted BCO 43-4 requirement for written notice of appeal within 15 days of adjournment, and BCO 43-10 regarding Kim's absence.
